propositions: Retired env vars and removed TOML keys are silently ignored or fall through to defaults, redirecting the store or weakening security/policy without any operator signal.
A RemovedInput tombstone registry raises a dedicated RemovedInputError subclassing BaseException so it cannot be swallowed by the ConfigError/broad-except handlers in tracker_dir() and MCP boot.
Error-class tombstones for store/security/policy/sync inputs must fail with nonzero exit naming old name, replacement, and removed-in release, while inert inputs only warn.

Env-var and file-presence tombstone checks are placed at the top of load_config() before the cache lookup, and TICKETS_TRACKER_DIR is gated in tracker_dir_override(), so they fire on warm-cache hits and every resolution path.
A rebar config validate / doctor command uses scan_tombstones() to collect all matches without raising and exits nonzero on any error-class match.
A CI golden-file guard fails if a recognised config key or env var is removed without a corresponding tombstone entry, keeping the registry complete.
